name: Pecan Whole Wheat Bread
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Toasted pecans, broken into large pieces
1 cup Rye flour (or an additional 1 cup whole wheat)
3 cups Bread flour to 4 cups all-purpose flour
recipeInstructions:
1. Proof yeast in warm milk in a large bowl.
2. Add molasses, oil, salt, and pecan pieces; mix together.
3. Mix in whole wheat and rye flour.
4. Gradually add bread flour (1/2 cup at a time) until dough is stiff.
5. Remove dough from the bowl onto a well-floured surface and knead gently to make a soft dough.
6. Place dough in lightly oiled bowl, cover, and let rise until double.
7. Punch down dough; divide into two equal parts.
8. Form each part into a loaf and place in greased 9x5-inch or 8x4-inch bread pans.
9. Let rise until doubled.
10. Bake in a preheated oven at 350 degrees F for 35 minutes.
11. Remove from pans; continue baking until the loaves sound hollow when tapped on the bottom.
12. Cool on racks before cutting. If needed, cover tops with aluminum foil during final 10-15 minutes of baking to prevent excessive browning.
